reforms.description: Denmark is implementing a comprehensive green R&D strategy focused on four missions: carbon capture and storage, power-to-X, climate-friendly agriculture, and plastic waste reduction. Major funding includes DKK 3.9 billion allocated in the COVID-19 recovery plan for green technology R&D, with DKK 543 million in new EUDP grants in 2021 (over DKK 200 million for CCS and power-to-X). Multiple demonstration projects are underway including Europe's largest CO2-free ammonia production facility and sustainable fuel production near Copenhagen.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investments in clean technology R&D and demonstration projects will enhance productivity through technological innovation, create new export opportunities in green technologies, and improve resource efficiency
reforms.status_evidence: DKK 3.9 billion allocated in COVID-19 recovery plan, DKK 543 million in EUDP grants disbursed in 2021, demonstration projects operating
reforms.importance_rationale: Multi-billion DKK investment representing a structural shift in Denmark's innovation policy toward green technologies, with comprehensive strategy covering multiple sectors and technology areas
reforms.source_quote: Major energy-related research and development programmes include the Energy Technology Development and Demonstration Programme (EUDP), the Innovation Fund and ELForsk, which mainly provide support to projects implemented in public-private partnerships. Support stepped up in 2020

reforms.description: Denmark allocated DKK 6 billion to the Danish Green Investment Fund in September 2020, an independent state loan fund offering risk capital for green transition across five areas: food/agriculture, energy/utilities, building/infrastructure, materials/resources, and transport/mobility. The Green recovery plan includes up to DKK 700 million in grants for investment in green technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Risk capital provision addresses market failures in green technology financing, enabling private sector innovation and accelerating deployment of productivity-enhancing clean technologies
reforms.status_evidence: Fund allocated DKK 6 billion in September 2020
reforms.importance_rationale: DKK 6 billion allocation represents a major new financing instrument for green innovation, addressing systemic gaps in risk capital for clean technology development
reforms.source_quote: In September 2020, a trans-partisan agreement between the Danish Government and other parties allocated DKK 6 billion to the Danish Green Investment Fund, an independent state loan fund that offers risk capital to promote a green transition.

reforms.description: Denmark temporarily enhanced its R&D tax allowance to 130% for R&D expenses up to DKK 850 million for 2020-2022 as part of COVID-19 stimulus measures, expanding support for business R&D through the tax system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhanced tax incentives for R&D reduce the cost of private innovation activities, encouraging firms to increase R&D spending and productivity-enhancing innovation
reforms.status_evidence: Tax allowance increased to 130% in 2020 and extended through 2022
reforms.importance_rationale: Significant but temporary enhancement of existing R&D tax incentive scheme, not a structural reform
reforms.source_quote: The package also included a temporary increase in the R&D enhanced tax allowance to 130% in 2020 and 2021 (later extended to 2022) for R&D expenses up to DKK 850 million.

reforms.description: Denmark is encouraging investment in electric vehicle charging infrastructure, particularly in remote areas, to support the transition to low and zero-carbon vehicles.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Infrastructure investment enables adoption of cleaner transport technologies, reducing energy costs and improving productivity through better transport networks
reforms.status_evidence: Text indicates Denmark 'is encouraging investment' suggesting ongoing policy
reforms.importance_rationale: Important infrastructure development but focused on deployment rather than innovation
reforms.source_quote: Continue to encourage the shift towards low and zero-carbon vehicles, including with incentives to invest in recharging stations particularly in remote areas.

reforms.description: Denmark announced a new digitalisation strategy to be launched in early 2022 based on recommendations from the Digitization Partnership released in October 2021.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Digital transformation strategies typically enhance productivity through improved business processes and technology adoption
reforms.status_evidence: Strategy to be launched in early 2022
reforms.importance_rationale: New national strategy but specific innovation content unclear - classified as 'other' due to lack of clear R&D or innovation instrument details
reforms.source_quote: A digital growth reform package was introduced in 2018 and a new digitalisation strategy will be launched in early 2022 on the basis of of the Digitization Partnership (2021) recommendations released in October 2021.

reforms.description: Denmark should provide more support to green investment and R&D as part of recommendations to bring the current account closer to balance through sustainable increases in public and private investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding would enhance innovation capacity and productivity growth
reforms.importance_rationale: General recommendation without specific policy details or funding amounts
reforms.source_quote: A sustainable increase in public and private investment would bring the current account closer to balance and several recommendations in this Survey push in this direction, including shifting towards less distortionary taxation that creates less disincentives to investment, providing more support to green investment and R&D

reforms.description: Denmark expanded support measures to provide more equity finance and access to capital for young firms in the scale-up phase, with recommendations to reduce barriers to digital trade and increase adoption of digital technologies to boost firm productivity by around half a per cent.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Support for scale-ups and digital adoption directly targets productivity growth, with quantified impact of 0.5% productivity increase
reforms.status_evidence: Text uses 'should expand' and 'expanded' suggesting mix of recommendations and actions
reforms.importance_rationale: Expansion of existing support measures rather than new programme
reforms.source_quote: Expand support for young firms to scale-up and reduce barriers to digital trade each have the potential to increase firm productivity in Denmark by around half a per cent through increasing adoption of digital technologies

reforms.description: Denmark should increase venture capital and private equity growth capital to match the average of the top 3 European countries, with half of additional financing assumed to be public.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased venture capital availability enables innovation commercialization and high-growth firm development
reforms.importance_rationale: Significant policy recommendation to match top European countries but not yet implemented
reforms.source_quote: More access to capital in the scale-up phase. Increase venture capital and private equity growth capital to match the average of the top 3 European countries

reforms.description: Denmark has developed a new macroeconomic model called GreenReform to provide more disaggregated and dynamic information on environmental and climate effects of economic policies. The project group consists of 15-20 people, supported by a government grant of DKK 14.3 million and DKK 6.55 million from other sources.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Better policy modeling tools enable more effective environmental and economic policies, supporting sustainable growth
reforms.status_evidence: Model 'has been developed' suggesting ongoing work
reforms.importance_rationale: Useful analytical tool but relatively small investment (DKK 20.85 million total)
reforms.source_quote: The development of a new macroeconomic model, GreenReform, will support this approach by providing more disaggregated and dynamic information on the environmental and climate effects of economic policies... The project group developing the model is comprised of 15-20 people, supported by a government grant of DKK 14.3 million and DKK 6.55 million in funding from other sources.
